large seed.Message: You collect the large watermelon seed.

thin seed.Message: You collect the thin watermelon seed.

round seed.Message: You collect the round watermelon seed.

tiny seed.Message: You collect the tiny watermelon seed.

sky lantern.Message: A sky lantern rises from the mist and begins to drift beside you.

Bedroom in the Afternoon.Body: You ate too much watermelon in the afternoon and fell asleep before the sweetness fully left your mouth.

Dream Start.Body: You are holding onto a white loong as it lifts slowly from the ground. Secretly, you begin to wonder whether it would make a good form of transportation. The loong is gentle with people. It drifts through the air in a way that lets you feel almost no motion at all, as if it is deliberately trying not to jolt you. Even so, you keep both arms tightly around part of its body, your face and hands pressed against the hard bristles along its back.as it glides through the air with impossible smoothness. Below you, lakes lie scattered across mountains, plains, reeds, and falling water.

On the White Loong.Body: You keep holding the white loong as it glides through the air with impossible smoothness. Below you, lakes lie scattered across mountains, plains, reeds, and falling water.

Choices.Text: Stop trying to direct the loong

Mountain Lake.Body: Far below the mountain, a dark lake rests in a bowl of stone. A large watermelon seed drifts on the surface like a tiny boat.

Waterfall Lake.Body: Mist gathers where the waterfall strikes the lake. A thin watermelon seed spins in a slow circle near the foam.

Plain Lake.Body: This lake lies in the open plain, wide and quiet under pale light. A round watermelon seed floats in the middle of a still reflection.

Reed Lake.Body: Among the reeds, the lake looks almost hidden. A tiny watermelon seed catches in the shadows between two stalks.
Reed Lake.DarkBody: The reeds are too dense and the water is too dark. You cannot see what is drifting there.

Skyward Rush.Body: Once you return with every kind of seed, the white loong dives into the water and bursts upward in a single impossible motion. The world falls away. You lose your grip. You are falling too.

At the Loong's Whim.Body: You loosen your grip and stop trying to direct it. The loong seems pleased by that. It circles once, as if deciding which part of the dream deserves you next.

Dark Bedroom.Body: You wake all at once. The room is dark now, fully dark, and the taste of watermelon is faint but still there.

Balcony at Night.Body: You step onto the balcony and look up. At that exact moment, a long meteor crosses the sky, white and distant, like the loong from your dream.
